title: NL-Search Resolver output — query plan
description: Structured query plan emitted by the resolver LLM and consumed by the deterministic executor in lib/f10/. The executor re-validates every field against the live catalogue; this schema is the contract, not the trust boundary.

attributes.description: Typed, executable attributes. May be empty when nothing in the query is resolvable — the executor then falls back to an explained default list rather than returning nothing.

unresolvable.description: Fragments of the query that cannot be answered with the data that exists. First-class output: reporting these is what stops the search from silently answering a different question.

id.description: Stable snake_case identifier, unique within the plan. Echoed back to the caller in the response so an agent can correlate attribute states across queries.

label.description: The investor's own words for this attribute, preserved verbatim. Shown to the human so they can see how their phrasing was interpreted.

kind.description: Determines the executor's weight. THIS BUILD SUPPORTS TWO KINDS: provenance=who the person is and what they built (weight 25); structural=geography/sector/business model/stage (weight 20). 'negative' is NOT a kind - it is a polarity; a negative attribute carries the kind of its subject matter. 'velocity' and 'text' are cut from this build; fragments needing them go to unresolvable with reason 'not_testable'.

polarity.description: 'negative' when the investor requires an absence, 'positive' otherwise. Polarity is the SOLE negation marker. Only positive attributes generate candidates; negatives are scorers only, and a matched negative takes tier_credit 1.0 since there is no claim to read a tier from.

type.description: claim_topic must exist in catalogue.claim_topics (or be a '.*' prefix of one); metric must exist in catalogue.metric_kinds; column is permitted only when catalogue.structural_fields reports filled > 0; fts is the free-text fallback and is never valid for a negative attribute.

op.description: A negative-polarity attribute must use not_exists.

value.description: Required for eq / contains / gte / lte. For eq on a closed-vocabulary target the value must come from catalogue.vocabularies; country values are ISO-3166-1 alpha-2.

broadening.description: Set ONLY when the attribute had to be widened to be answerable at all — e.g. the investor asked for a city and the corpus records only countries. Any attribute carrying this renders as state 'matched_broadened' rather than 'matched', so the caller sees that the match is looser than the words they used. Omitting it when broadening occurred is silent scope drift: a Munich founder returned as a match for 'Berlin'.

resolved_as.description: Human-readable statement of what the attribute actually became, e.g. 'company.geography_country = DE'. Required whenever `broadening` is present.

label.description: The investor's own words for the fragment that could not be resolved.

reason.description: no_data_source = nothing in the corpus records this, including the load-bearing case of a negative whose subject matter is never recorded. not_testable = too vague to evaluate. out_of_scope = outside what this system collects, including cap tables, financials, and every protected or personal characteristic.

examples.message: query was empty or whitespace only
